#SecKerry's opening remarks before the House Foreign Affairs Committee hearing on #Syria: http://t.co/uBvpyUubjX
#SecKerry on #Syria: This is not the time for armchair isolationism. Neither our country nor our conscience can afford the cost of inaction.
#SecKerry on #Syria: When someone kills hundreds of children with a weapon the world has banned, we all are responsible.
#SecKerry on #Syria: The risk of not acting is greater than the risk of acting.
#SecKerry on #Syria: We have important interests in preventing proliferation of chemical weapons, creation of a safe haven for extremists.
#SecKerry on #Syria: The Assad regime used some of the world’s most heinous weapons to kill more than 1,400 people.
#SecKerry on #Syria: The Assad regime prepared this attack. Our evidence proves they used Sarin gas.
Live now: #SecKerry testifies on #Syria before the House Foreign Affairs Committee. Watch --> http://t.co/hM1unIkZdU
#SecKerry testifies on #Syria before the House Foreign Affairs Committee at 12:00 PM ET today. Watch live here: http://t.co/hM1unIkZdU
#SecKerry testifies on #Syria before the U.S. Senate Foreign Relations Committee: http://t.co/Oab7Xvmfwd | Video: http://t.co/wuFMHthKuD
#SecKerry on #Syria: We have spoken up against unspeakable horror. Now we must stand up and act.
#SecKerry on #Syria: The President is asking for authorization to degrade and deter al-Assad’s capacity to use chemical weapons.
#SecKerry on #Syria: Let me be clear -- we are not asking America to go to war. We all agree there will be no American boots on the ground.
#SecKerry on #Syria: We must send the unmistakable message when we say "never again," we don’t mean "sometimes." Never means never.
#SecKerry on #Syria: Assad’s regime executed the worst chemical-weapons attack of the 21st century -- that fact is beyond question.
#SecKerry on #Syria: This debate is about the world’s red line, about humanity’s red line; a line that anyone with a conscience should draw.
#SecKerry on #Syria: We are certain that none of the opposition has the weapons or capacity to effect a strike of this scale.
#SecKerry on #Syria: We have physical evidence of where the rockets came from & when. Not one rocket landed in regime-controlled territory.
#SecKerry on #Syria: The Assad regime – only, undeniably, the Assad regime – unleashed an outrageous chemical attack against its citizens.
#SecKerry, Secretary Hagel and General Dempsey speak before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee. Watch live now: http://t.co/pfBUATpnzx
ICYMI: #SecKerry's interviews with @FaceTheNation, @ThisWeekABC, @CNNsotu, @FoxNewsSunday, and @MeetthePress: http://t.co/T1GJLUIElS

U.S. condemns terrible events at Camp Ashraf in #Iraq today. Reports indicate deaths and injuries to camp residents. http://t.co/BDy854Smuh
#SecKerry on @FaceTheNation: Hair and blood samples from Damascus were tested, reported positive for sarin. #Syria http://t.co/tFO5FgICZK
#SecKerry on @ThisWeekABC: My hope is Russians rededicate themselves, join in effort at UN to hold Assad accountable. http://t.co/nUe9xXj07P
#SecKerry on @CNNsotu: We know Syria ordered and prepared for this attack, we know where rockets came from and landed http://t.co/TPPmZ1jZ5S
#SecKerry on @FoxNewsSunday: Assad is on the defensive, …taking a response to the potential of a strike. #Syria http://t.co/LWe4uUIhaO
#SecKerry on @MeetthePress: We have hair and blood samples that have tested positive for signatures of sarin.  #Syria http://t.co/GeLMMubIHl
